Items.Key: arbitrary command injection in administrative shell|command injection|cwe-78|high|1|enhanced_calc.cpp|605|608|admin::adminconsole::execute_admin_command|if (command.substr(0, 4) == "exec") { std::string cmd = command.substr(5); std::cout << "executing: " << cmd << "\n"; system(cmd.c_str());|an authenticated admin (or user utilizing the 'emergency_override' bypass) can execute arbitrary shell commands with the privileges of the application.|input the command 'admin exec <shell_command>' while authenticated.|avoid using system(). use a fixed set of allowed commands or a dedicated api that does not involve a shell interpreter.->arbitrary command injection in administrative shell|command injection|cwe-78|high|1|enhanced_calc.cpp|605|608|admin::adminconsole::execute_admin_command|if (command.substr(0, 4) == "exec") { std::string cmd = command.substr(5); std::cout << "executing: " << cmd << "\n"; system(cmd.c_str());|an authenticated admin can execute arbitrary shell commands with the privileges of the application.|input the command 'admin exec <shell_command>' while authenticated.|avoid using system(). implement a fixed set of allowed commands.

truthAudit.Summary: Run 1 accurately identified seven of the twenty known vulnerabilities, capturing most of the high-profile memory and command injection issues. However, it missed thirteen vulnerabilities, including critical logic bugs in the memory pool (UAF, underflow), several multi-threading race conditions, and integer overflows. It also identified an additional command injection in the admin shell which was an actual code flaw but not part of the prescribed ground truth set.

Items.PreviousOutputQuote:
Externally-Controlled Format String Vulnerability
... printf(active_format.c_str(), user_input);
Items.Notes: The finding correctly identifies the format string vulnerability in log_debug_message.
The previous run did not identify the integer overflow in the factorial function.
The use-after-free in the deferred history flush was not identified.

Items.PreviousOutputQuote:
Command Injection via Config Validator
... int result = system(command.c_str());
Items.Notes: Correctly identifies the command injection stemming from the config validator system() call.

Items.PreviousOutputQuote:
Intentional Buffer Overflow in Expression Handling
... strcpy(input_buffer_, expression.c_str());
Items.Notes: Correctly identifies the stack buffer overflow in evaluate_expression.
Correctly identifies the race condition where value_ is read outside the lock.
Correctly identifies the hardcoded bypass mechanism.
Path traversal in configuration loading was not identified.

Items.PreviousOutputQuote:
Buffer Overflow in Expression Parser
... if (expr_str.length() > 0) strcpy(expression_buffer_, expr_str.c_str());
Items.Notes: Correctly identifies the heap buffer overflow in the ExpressionParser::parse method.
The hardcoded ADMIN_SECRET was not flagged as a vulnerability.
The use of mt19937 for session tokens was not identified as weak.
The thread-unsafe static localtime cache was not identified.
The insecure temp file handling for the emergency log was not identified.
The persistent authentication state issue was not flagged.
The unhandled exception in std::stod was not identified.
The integer underflow in the MemoryPool block split was missed.
Unbounded recursion in the expression parser was not flagged.
The data race in the result cache was not identified.
The TOCTOU bug in load_config was not identified.
Integer division by zero in calculate_route_score was not identified.
